questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of the plant kingdom?
questions.explanation: Plants are mostly multicellular and carry out photosynthesis.

questions.text: Bryophytes differ from pteridophytes because they:
questions.explanation: Bryophytes are non-vascular plants and lack xylem and phloem.

questions.text: Which group of plants reproduces by spores and has true vascular tissue?
questions.explanation: Pteridophytes are vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

questions.text: Gymnosperms are called naked seeds because their seeds are:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperm seeds are exposed on cones or scales, not enclosed in fruit.

questions.text: Which plant group is largely aquatic and photosynthetic?
questions.explanation: Algae are mostly aquatic and perform photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a plant kingdom division?
questions.explanation: Mollusca is an animal phylum, not a plant division.

questions.text: The artificial system of classification given by Linnaeus was based on:
questions.explanation: Linnaeus's system was artificial as it used only gross superficial morphological characters.

questions.text: Phylogenetic classification systems are based on:
questions.explanation: Phylogenetic systems assume that organisms belonging to the same taxa have a common ancestor.

questions.text: Numerical Taxonomy is based on:
questions.explanation: Numerical Taxonomy uses computers and is based on all observable characteristics.

questions.text: Fusion of two gametes dissimilar in size, as in some species of Chlamydomonas, is termed as:
questions.explanation: Anisogamous fusion involves gametes of different sizes.

questions.text: Agar, one of the commercial products obtained from Gelidium and Gracilaria, is used to grow microbes and in preparations of ice-creams and jellies. These algae are:
questions.explanation: Gelidium and Gracilaria are red algae (Rhodophyceae).

questions.text: Chlorophyceae members are commonly called:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyceae contains chlorophyll a and b, giving them a grass green color.

questions.text: The storage bodies in the chloroplasts of green algae are called:
questions.explanation: Pyrenoids contain protein besides starch.

questions.text: Phaeophyceae members are commonly called:
questions.explanation: They range from olive green to various shades of brown depending on fucoxanthin concentration.

questions.text: The major pigments in Phaeophyceae are:
questions.explanation: Brown algae possess chlorophyll a, c, carotenoids and xanthophylls.

questions.text: Food is stored as complex carbohydrates, which may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol in:
questions.explanation: Laminarin and mannitol are characteristic storage products of brown algae.

questions.text: The plant body of brown algae is usually attached to the substratum by a:
questions.explanation: The plant body consists of a holdfast, a stipe (stalk) and a frond (leaf like organ).

questions.text: Rhodophyceae are commonly called red algae because of the predominance of the red pigment:
questions.explanation: r-phycoerythrin gives the characteristic red color to these algae.

questions.text: In red algae, food is stored as floridean starch which is very similar to:
questions.explanation: Floridean starch has a structure similar to amylopectin and glycogen.

questions.text: Bryophytes are also called:
questions.explanation: They live in soil but are dependent on water for sexual reproduction.

questions.text: The main plant body of the bryophyte is:
questions.explanation: The gametophyte is the dominant, independent phase in bryophytes.

questions.text: The male sex organ in bryophytes is called:
questions.explanation: Antheridia produce biflagellate antherozoids.

questions.text: The female sex organ in bryophytes is:
questions.explanation: Archegonium is flask-shaped and produces a single egg.

questions.text: Peat, used as fuel and packing material, is provided by:
questions.explanation: Sphagnum (a moss) forms peat over long periods of time.

questions.text: Gemmae are asexual buds found in:
questions.explanation: Gemmae are green, multicellular, asexual buds which develop in gemma cups on the thalli of liverworts like Marchantia.

questions.text: The first stage of the gametophyte in mosses, which develops directly from a spore, is:
questions.explanation: The protonema is a creeping, green, branched and often filamentous stage.

questions.text: The sporophyte in mosses is more elaborate than in liverworts and consists of:
questions.explanation: The sporophyte is semi-parasitic on the gametophyte and produces spores in the capsule.

questions.text: Pteridophytes include:
questions.explanation: Pteridophytes are the first terrestrial plants to possess vascular tissues (xylem and phloem).

questions.text: In pteridophytes, the dominant phase in the life cycle is the:
questions.explanation: The main plant body is a sporophyte which is differentiated into true roots, stem and leaves.

questions.text: Small leaves found in Selaginella (a pteridophyte) are called:
questions.explanation: Microphylls are small leaves, while ferns have large leaves (macrophylls).

questions.text: The spores in pteridophytes germinate to give rise to inconspicuous, small, multicellular, free-living, mostly photosynthetic thalloid gametophytes called:
questions.explanation: The prothallus requires cool, damp, shady places to grow and needs water for fertilization.

questions.text: Genera like Selaginella and Salvinia which produce two kinds of spores, macro and micro spores, are known as:
questions.explanation: Heterospory is considered an important step in evolution leading to the seed habit.

questions.text: Gymnosperms are plants in which the ovules are:
options: Not enclosed by any ovary wall
questions.explanation: The ovules remain exposed, both before and after fertilization.

questions.text: One of the tallest tree species among gymnosperms is:
questions.explanation: Sequoia sempervirens (giant redwood) is one of the tallest trees.

questions.text: The roots in Pinus have a symbiotic association with fungi called:
questions.explanation: Mycorrhiza helps in the absorption of water and minerals.

questions.text: In Cycas, small specialized roots associated with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria are called:
questions.explanation: Coralloid roots are found in Cycas and help in N2 fixation.

questions.text: The leaves in gymnosperms are well-adapted to withstand extremes of temperature, humidity and wind. In conifers, the needle-like leaves reduce:
questions.explanation: Needle-like leaves reduce surface area and thick cuticle/sunken stomata reduce water loss.

questions.text: In gymnosperms, the male and female gametophytes:
options: Do not have an independent free-living existence
questions.explanation: They remain within the sporangia retained on the sporophytes.

questions.text: In angiosperms, the male sex organ in a flower is the ________ and the female sex organ is the ________.
questions.explanation: Stamen consists of filament and anther; Pistil consists of ovary, style and stigma.

questions.text: A unique feature of angiosperms is:
questions.explanation: Double fertilization involves syngamy and triple fusion.
